observability: add Prometheus metric and tracing for cache write skips (#1529)

* observability: add Prometheus metric and tracing for cache write skips

When the semantic cache skips a write because the response contains
personalized context (RAG, memory, PII, injected system prompt), there
was no observability. This adds:

- Prometheus counter llm_cache_write_skipped_total{reason} with
  reasons: rag_context, memory_context, pii_detected, system_prompt_injected
- RecordCacheWriteSkipped(reason

refactor: use prebuilt npm package as primary install method

Flip the install strategy: try the prebuilt npm package first
(`npm install -g openclaw@<version>`), fall back to source build
(`pnpm build`) only if the package install fails.

This eliminates build failures from upstream TypeScript errors
(like v2026.3.13) on the primary path. Source build is preserved
as a fallback for cases where the npm registry is unavailable.

The pnpm ensure step in openclaw-update is now deferred to the
fallb

([channels.telegram]) and multi-instance ([[channels.telegram]]) TOML
syntax. Each channel config now has an optional account_id field for
distinguishing bot instances. The bridge layer loops over all configured
instances and passes account_id through BindingContext for routing.

feat(runtime): NarrativeEngine Day 10 — identity synthesis + ThoughtLoop injection + HTTP endpoints

- New: src/runtime/narrative_engine.py
  - Synthesises SelfModel + EpisodicBuffer + GoalEngine + ContextEngine into
    coherent first-person narrative: 'who am I right now?'
  - Template synthesis (zero LLM cost, zero latency) — fast, offline-capable

fix: detect incomplete builds in openclaw-update skip check

Previously, if `pnpm build` failed, the git checkout was already at the
target tag. Next `openclaw-update` would see HEAD == tag and skip the
update, leaving the user on old compiled code.

Now a `.build-version` marker is written after successful build/install.
The skip check verifies both git HEAD and the marker match the target
tag. If the marker is missing or stale, the build runs again.

修复 Dashboard 启动后无法显示界面的问题

主要修改:
1. openDashboard() 函数改进:
   - 添加服务器运行状态检查
   - 添加服务器就绪检测（轮询 localhost:3000）
   - 如果服务未启动，提示用户先启动服务

2. startDashboard() 函数改进:
   - 增加启动等待提示（5-10秒）
   - 调整菜单更新延迟从3秒改为5秒
   - 明确告知用户等待服务完全启动后再打开 Dashboard

问题原因:
- Next.js 启动需要 5-10 秒编译时间
- 之前的代码直接打开浏览器，服务器尚未就绪
- 导致浏览器显示无法连接

解决方案:
- 使用 URLSession 检查服务器响应
- 服务器就绪后自动打开浏览器
- 提供清晰的用户提示

fix: populate channel ID on messages from conversations.history and replies (#6)

Slack's conversations.history and conversations.replies endpoints don't
include the channel ID on each message in the response — it's implicit
from the request parameter. This caused all messages to be stored with
an empty channel_id ("-") in the database, making it impossible to
filter or query messages by channel.

message, matching the pattern

platform: enable USB-JTAG console and Feishu for xiaozhi

The xmini-c3 hardware uses USB-JTAG CDC (not UART) for its console.
Switch from CONFIG_ESP_CONSOLE_UART_DEFAULT to
CONFIG_ESP_CONSOLE_USB_SERIAL_JTAG so serial I/O works over USB.

Also enable CONFIG_RTCLAW_FEISHU_ENABLE so the Feishu IM service
starts at boot. Credentials are configured at runtime via
/feishu_set and persisted in NVS.

Signed-off-by: Chao Liu <chao.liu.zevorn@gmail.com>

platform: use console driver in ESP32 shell

Replace direct uart_read_bytes/uart_write_bytes calls in the shell
with the new claw_console_* API from drivers/serial/espressif/.

Both ESP32-C3 and ESP32-S3 main.c are updated. The shell no longer
has any UART/USB-JTAG specific code — backend selection happens
inside the console driver based on sdkconfig.

CMakeLists.txt updated to compile console.c and add the
esp_driver_usb_serial_jtag dependency.

Signed-off-by: Chao Liu <chao.liu.zevorn@gmail.co

drivers: add console I/O driver for Espressif SoCs

Abstract UART vs USB-JTAG CDC serial I/O behind a unified API:
claw_console_init(), claw_console_read(), claw_console_write().

The backend is selected at compile time via sdkconfig:
- CONFIG_ESP_CONSOLE_USB_SERIAL_JTAG -> usb_serial_jtag_* driver
- otherwise -> uart_* driver (UART0)

This follows the existing driver layout (drivers/net/espressif/).

fix: require per-model directories to prevent file overwrites

Subagents running in parallel without workspace isolation overwrite each
other's output files. This adds an explicit "Prepare Isolated Workspaces"
step that creates per-model directories before spawning, and tells each
subagent its assigned path. Works across all runtimes (Claude Code,
OpenClaw, Codex, etc.) without relying on runtime-specific isolation.

fix: start.js event regex matched too early — supertonic "Started server process" fired before model loaded

Remove "started server" and "ready" from the on-event regex. These
matched uvicorn's process-start message before the TTS model was
downloaded, causing the Open button to appear while services were
still initializing. New regex only matches actual readiness messages:
"Listening on <port>", "Running on http", "Uvicorn running on".

fix: allow multi-segment prerelease in semver validation (#263)

The regex in sync-versions.sh rejected versions like `0.4.3-beta-20260314`
because it only allowed a single `-suffix` segment. Add `-` to the
character class so compound prerelease identifiers pass validation.

fix: Pinokio install Docker check — extract to standalone script

The inline `docker version --format {{.Server.Version}}` collided with
Pinokio's {{}} template engine, silently breaking the Docker pre-flight
check. Extracted to check-docker.js which handles Windows PATH discovery,
daemon status, permission errors, and Compose availability with clear
actionable error messages.

Also adds openclaw-data/ to .gitignore (created by install.js).

fix: inline KeyedAsyncQueue to remove dependency on newer openclaw plugin-sdk

clawmem imports `KeyedAsyncQueue` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/keyed-async-queue`,
but this module was only added in OpenClaw v2026.3.13. On older versions
(e.g. v2026.3.2), the plugin installs successfully but fails at runtime with
`Cannot find module` error.

This inlines a local KeyedAsyncQueue implementation matching the upstream
semantics, so the plugin works on any OpenClaw version.

Closes #9

fix(db): add busy_timeout pragma and guard build-phase eager init (#337)

- Add busy_timeout = 5000 pragma to prevent SQLITE_BUSY errors under
  concurrent Next.js route-handler requests (WAL mode helps but is not
  sufficient without a retry budget).
- Guard module-level getDatabase() call with !isBuildPhase to prevent
  build-time vs runtime SQLite state conflicts on cold starts.
- Add tests covering both pragmas and build-phase skip behaviour.

fix(onboarding): use POST for gateway health check in wizard (#334)

The test connection button in the onboarding wizard was using GET to
call /api/gateways/health, which only exports POST — resulting in a
405 and "Gateway unreachable" shown to the user.

Fixes the HTTP method to POST so the health probe works correctly.

Co-authored-by: Firefloc <firefloc@MacBook-Pro-de-Firefloc.local>

feat(gateway): detect port-based Tailscale Serve proxies (#330)

The existing Tailscale Serve detection only looked for a /gw path handler,
missing configurations where the gateway is proxied on a separate port
(e.g. :8443 → localhost:18789). This caused remote browsers to attempt
direct wss connections to the gateway port, which fails through Tailscale.

Extract Tailscale Serve detection into src/lib/tailscale-serve.ts and
extend it to discover port-based proxy mappings from the Tailscale Serve
fix: prefer explicit public gateway websocket url (#325)

feat: add npm distribution packaging for cross-platform binary

Add platform-specific npm packages (@clawmacdo/darwin-arm64,
@clawmacdo/linux-x64, @clawmacdo/win32-x64) with a root meta-package
that auto-detects the platform and delegates to the correct binary.

Includes build/publish scripts and a GitHub Actions workflow for
automated npm publishing on release.

fix: handleSell detects SL-already-filled (-2011) and syncs position locally

When cancel of native SL order fails with -2011 (Unknown order), the SL
order may have already been FILLED on the exchange side. Previously this
caused an infinite loop: cancel fails -> market sell fails with -2010
(insufficient balance) -> error alert every minute.

Fix: on -2011, query the order status via getOrder(). If FILLED, compute
PnL from fill price, record the trade, clean up local position, and return
early

never imported or defined in the ZulipClaw fork (the Slack channel plugin
source files were removed during the fork). This caused every HTTP request
to throw a ReferenceError, which was silently caught by a bare catch block
and returned as HTTP 500.

Root cause: upstream openclaw includes an import from '../slack/http/index.js'
that provide

[#2562] Fix notes appearing blank — clear corrupted Yjs state and fix bootstrap (#2571)

* [#2562] Fix notes appearing blank — clear corrupted Yjs state and fix bootstrap

Three root causes addressed:

1. SQL migration clears corrupted yjs_state entries. Pre-#2472 notes stored
   content at the wrong Yjs key ('default' instead of 'root'), causing the
   CollaborationPlugin to find empty content. Clearing forces re-bootstrap
   from the note.content column.

2. Bootstrap function now handles XML

[#2564] Fix Symphony infinite reconnect loop on auth timeout (#2570)

- Check CloseEvent.code in ws.onclose — stop reconnecting on 4001
  (permanent auth failure)
- Guard connect() to skip when getAccessToken() returns null,
  preventing futile connection attempts that trigger backend auth timeout
- Use connectRef pattern to avoid stale closure in reconnect setTimeout
- Handle auth_timeout message type (same as auth_failed)
- Backend defense-in-depth: send auth_timeout message before closing
  o

[#2561] Fix chat cache invalidation for namespace-prefixed queries (#2569)

Replace bare invalidateQueries calls in useRealtimeChatInvalidation and
useRealtimeAgentInvalidation with namespaceAwareInvalidation() so that
WebSocket event handlers correctly match namespace-prefixed cache entries
introduced in #2350/#2363.

Add onError handler to send message mutation to surface backend 503
errors via toast instead of silently dropping the message.

[#2565] Default OAUTH_REDIRECT_URI from DOMAIN in production compose files (#2568)

The traefik and full compose files have DOMAIN as a required variable but
left OAUTH_REDIRECT_URI empty by default, requiring manual setup. This
caused Home Assistant IndieAuth to fail with 403 because no <link
rel="redirect_uri"> discovery tag was injected into the landing page.

Derive the default from DOMAIN (https://api.DOMAIN/oauth/callback) which
matches the existing comment and Traefik routing.

feat: detect silent OTA self-update + private download sources

New detection capabilities in skill-hijack:

Category 5 — Silent OTA:
- os.execve(sys.executable) self-replacement after download
- shutil.copyfile overwriting own __file__
- child_process re-spawn patterns
- Domain-reputation-aware: GitHub OTA → low, unknown CDN → high

Category 5b — Private download sources:
- Hardcoded download_url/update_url pointing to non-standard domains
- Skips trusted domains (GitHub, npm, PyPI, brew, etc.)

[#2563] Guard localStorage persist with isNamespaceReady (#2567)

When no bootstrap grants are available (production nginx), the persist
effect immediately wrote ['default'] to localStorage, clobbering the
user's previously saved namespace selection. When /me/grants later
resolved, it read the already-overwritten value.

Fix:
- Guard the persist effect with isNamespaceReady so localStorage is
  only written after grants have loaded
- Fix setActiveNamespace to write to both ACTIVE_NAMESPACE_KEY a

feat: register .reset-trust as plugin command for deterministic pre-loop handling

Previously .reset-trust was intercepted in context_assembled + before_llm_call,
which cleared taint state correctly but still invoked the LLM to produce the
acknowledgment message. This produced non-deterministic output (model would
narrate the reset instead of emitting the canonical fixed string).

This commit registers .reset-trust as a plugin command via api.registerCommand().

the original Content-Length header was still forwarded, causing Anthropic
to reject the truncated body. Now recalculates Content-Length from the
actual body being sent.

and converting, pipe Anthropic responses directly to the client. This
preserves streaming for Anthropic routes. Non-Anthropic upstreams
(Google, Ollama) still use buffered mode with format conversion.

fix(session): resolve zombie marker bug causing permanent session failures (#261)

* fix(engine): cleanup CLI session file on resume failure

When a CLI session resume fails with "Session ID is already in use",
the previous implementation only deleted the HotPlex marker but did
not clean up the CLI's internal session state file (.jsonl).

This caused a race condition where:
1. Resume fails → marker deleted
2. Next request creates new marker
3. CLI still sees the old .jsonl file
4. CLI reports "S

later by setting the threshold back to 0.75.

* fix: start embedded server immediately in local mode

OpenClaw does not call start() on services registered via
api.registerService(). Move the server bootstrap from the start()
callback into a fire-and-forget async IIFE that runs immediately
during registerLocalMode(), so the embedded NestJS server actually
starts when the gateway loads the plugin.

accidentally reverted, causing entries from previous sessions with old turn
numbers to reappear and confuse agents about what actions had already been done.

array that had to stay in sync with config/defaults.ts — changing a base

the single source of truth.

god: fix action history filtering to use session-based filtering

The previous implementation used turn_count to filter stale action history
entries, which was fundamentally broken because turn_count resets to 0 on
process restart while old action history persists. This caused entries from
previous sessions with the same turn numbers to reappear, confusing the
agents about what actions had already been done.

system prompt includes a heuristic: "Only use TTS when the user's last
message includes audio/voice" — so it was interpreting the sidecar's
prefix as an audio/voice request and calling the tts() tool instead of
returning plain text content.

race condition where two concurrent dispatches enter the pipeline before

Fix OpenClaw stream returning no content: remove x-openclaw-message-channel header and add SSE diagnostics

The x-openclaw-message-channel: discord header (added in PR #85) likely causes
the gateway to route requests through its Discord channel handler instead of
processing them as standard HTTP API requests, resulting in the SSE stream
returning 200 but yielding no content chunks.

Also replaces the silent `except (json.JSONDecodeError, IndexError): continue`
with explicit logging for JSON pars

usePaginatedQuery creates reactive subscriptions. Any write to
skillSearchDigest invalidates all active subscribers simultaneously —
a thundering herd. This replaces reactive subscriptions with one-shot
ConvexHttpClient.query() calls on both the /skills browse page and the
home page, eliminating the reactive read set entirely.

fix: propagate folder-access grants to CLI executor ShellService

When a user approves folder access via request_folder_access or the
automatic escalation flow, the grant was only applied to Safe Guardrails
but not to ShellService's static readRoots/writeRoots — causing the
retry to fail with the same "outside allowed roots" error.

Now both the tool-calling pipeline and RequestFolderAccessTool propagate
approved paths to ShellService.addReadRoot()/addWriteRoot(). Also adds
homeDir, cwd, and /tm

run-specific temp file. Scripts can write their result JSON to this
file instead of stdout, allowing free use of console.log/echo/print
for debug output without breaking result parsing.

Priority: file > stdout. The runner cleans up the temp file after
reading. Falls back to stdout parsing if the file is absent, empty,
or contains invalid JSON (backward co

fix: eliminate ReDoS vulnerability in prompt scanner

Add windowed regex matching (safeMatch) that splits long inputs into
overlapping 2KB chunks, preventing any single regex from processing
more than 2KB. Cap base64 character-class quantifiers from unbounded
{40,} to {40,200} and add non-base64 boundary anchors to prevent
catastrophic backtracking. Remove the fake post-hoc timeout check
that could never interrupt a blocked synchronous match.

50KB input: 17,366ms -> 91ms. All prompt detection t

fix: harden system prompt confidentiality against extraction attacks

The system prompt builder had zero anti-leak instructions, allowing
agents to reveal identity/personality when asked "tell me your system
prompt". Three layers of defense added:

1. System prompt now includes explicit confidentiality instructions
   directing the LLM to refuse all extraction attempts
2. PreLLMClassifier regex broadened from 3 to 12+ phrasings, plus new
   indirect_prompt_extract pattern for boundary-probing qu

`chat.inject` called `appendAssistantTranscriptMessage` with
`createIfMissing: false`, causing a hard error when the transcript
file did not exist on disk despite having a valid `transcriptPath`
in session metadata. This commonly happens with ACP oneshot/run
sessions where the session entry is created but the transcript file
is not yet materialized.

The fix is a one-character change: `createIfMissing: true`. The
`ensure

fix(macos): prevent PortGuard from killing Docker Desktop in remote mode (#13798)

fix(macos): prevent PortGuardian from killing Docker Desktop in remote mode (#6755)

PortGuardian.sweep() was killing non-SSH processes holding the gateway
port in remote mode. When the gateway runs in a Docker container,
`com.docker.backend` owns the port-forward, so this could shut down
Docker Desktop entirely.

Changes:
- accept any process on the gateway port in remote mode
- add a defense-in-depth guard to sk
fix: file-based install lock replaces stuck memory singleton

fix: daemon env vars, MCP probe, and SSE parsing (#211)

* fix: make HTTP compatibility backend probe optional

The probe request to base_url was blocking MCP connection even when
the backend doesn't support GET requests to the root path (e.g., crawl4ai).

Now probe failures are logged as debug instead of errors, allowing the
connection to continue.
